other_observers: Many Observers during 6+ month stay; Yes
description: The bird was about 11 inches with black curved bill, yellowish-orange eye, irregular spotting on dirty white breast, buffy under tail, dark tail on top and dark underside and white toward tip. Gray bird with very small hint of wingbar if one at all. Tail and breast suggest race oberholseri but wingbars suggest palmeri.Behaviors: Bird would run with tail up at times into dense thicket. I watched the bird feed at the Red Rock's feeders eating sunflower and millet for almost 7 months. He would use his bill in a sweeping motion to search and dig for seed.Call: several chatters and whistles also a wit-wheet callPlumage: adult
